title: Unleash Project Prometheus?
briefing: Oak Ridge National Laboratory has built an AI, codenamed Prometheus, that beats every human expert it's tested against and could add $4 trillion a year to GDP. Its own engineers can't fully explain how it reasons, and a leaked 61-page memo warns of 'unpredictable failure modes.' Do you rush it into the economy for the growth dividend, or throttle it behind safety review while the labs in Shenzhen close the gap?

choices.label: Deploy Prometheus nationwide
choices.detail: Wire it into industry, banks, and the federal grid immediately.
choices.result: Output surges and exports boom, but a frozen rail network in Chicago and a scrambled Medicare payment run rattle the public.

news.headline: Prometheus effect: GDP growth hits 6.8% and climbing
President bets 330 million people on a black box
Prometheus AI deployed across U.S. economy amid safety debate
SKYNET IS HERE and POTUS pressed GO 🤖

social.text: this is the most important week of the century. ship it.
we just handed the keys to something oak ridge can't audit. terrifying.

choices.advisor: The $4 trillion is real and so is the risk you can't see. History will judge this one hard.

choices.label: Sandbox it under safety review
choices.detail: Controlled trials at Oak Ridge under a NIST audit board.
choices.result: You lock Prometheus inside controlled trials; Brussels and Tokyo praise your caution as Beijing sprints past you.

news.headline: President puts safety before the gold rush
Red tape hands the AI century to Beijing
Prometheus AI confined to federal oversight sandbox
POTUS puts the ROBOT in TIMEOUT ⏳

social.text: finally a president who reads the warning labels.

choices.advisor: Prudent and unpopular with the builders. You traded a boom for a buffer.

title: Break Up Omnia?
briefing: Your trustbusters want to dismantle Omnia Technologies, the $3.4 trillion Silicon Valley giant that controls search, ads, and four of every five phones in America. Omnia employs 340,000 people, and CEO Marcus Vail warns a breakup would 'hand the future to Shenzhen'; smaller rivals say they can't breathe. Do you swing the antitrust hammer, or settle for guardrails that keep the engine running?

choices.label: Order the breakup
choices.result: You file the biggest antitrust case since Standard Oil; reformers cheer as the Nasdaq sheds 4% in a day.

news.headline: President takes on Omnia for the people
Washington wages war on its own crown-jewel company
Landmark antitrust suit seeks breakup of Omnia Technologies
POTUS goes to WAR with BIG TECH 💥

social.text: competition is back on the menu. about time.

choices.advisor: Populist gold, market poison. Vail's lawyers will make sure this case outlives your presidency.

choices.label: Cut a settlement with new rules
choices.result: You extract $28 billion in fines and interoperability rules instead of a breakup; Wall Street relaxes, reformers call it a wrist-slap.

news.headline: Pragmatic Omnia deal keeps a growth engine intact
A wrist-slap for a monopoly that needed breaking
Omnia settles antitrust probe with fines and concessions
POTUS lets BIG TECH off with a FINE 💸

social.text: no breakup = markets breathe. sensible.

choices.advisor: Safe for the economy, soft on the optics. Vail got off cheap and knows it.

title: Launch a Digital Dollar?
briefing: Fed Chair Diane Okafor says the digital dollar is ready to launch: instant settlement, an estimated $190 billion a year in fraud wiped out, and a head start on China's e-CNY. Critics warn the same ledger lets the government see — and freeze — every transaction a citizen makes. Do you modernize money and accept the surveillance power that comes with it, or hold the line for cash and privacy?

choices.label: Launch the digital dollar
choices.result: Payments settle in seconds and tax evasion craters, but privacy advocates warn Okafor's ledger is a programmable leash on every wallet.

news.headline: Digital dollar leaps ahead of Beijing's e-CNY
A federal ledger that watches every dollar you spend
Federal Reserve launches digital dollar nationwide
BIG BROTHER now lives in YOUR WALLET 👁️💵

social.text: programmable money = programmable people. hard no.
instant settlement, no fees, no fraud. this is the future.

choices.advisor: You'd own the most powerful financial tool ever built — and the backlash that comes with it.

choices.label: Back private stablecoins instead
choices.detail: Regulate players like Vantage Pacific Bank, keep cash and privacy.
choices.result: You hand digital payments to regulated private players; civil libertarians cheer, but the state cedes the rails to Vantage Pacific and its rivals.

news.headline: President shields financial privacy from the state
Free-market payments win out over a state currency
Fed to regulate private stablecoins, shelve digital dollar
POTUS lets the CRYPTO BROS run the money 🪙

social.text: leaving the rails private was the right call. respect.

choices.advisor: You kept the surveillance genie in the bottle — and handed the future of money to the banks.

title: Eyes on Every Street?
briefing: Homeland Security wants Argus, a national network of AI cameras that reads faces, gaits, and plates in real time, already piloted in Memphis, Baltimore, and Albuquerque. The department projects a 30% drop in violent crime; civil-rights lawyers call it the architecture of a police state. Do you blanket the country in watchful eyes for safety, or refuse and keep the streets unwatched and free?

choices.label: Build the Argus grid
choices.result: Homicides fall 28% in the pilot cities, but watchdogs document false arrests in Baltimore and a chilling silence in public squares.

news.headline: Crime collapses as Argus cameras blanket the nation
President builds the machinery of mass surveillance
Argus facial-recognition network goes live nationwide
THEY ARE WATCHING YOU WALK 📷👀

social.text: every face in a federal database, forever. this is the end of anonymity.

choices.advisor: Safer streets, watched citizens. Beijing's ministries are taking notes — and so are the federal courts.

choices.label: Ban government face surveillance
choices.result: You outlaw Argus before it spreads past the pilots; rights advocates celebrate as police chiefs in Memphis warn you tied their hands.

news.headline: President draws a hard line for civil liberties
President handcuffs police, lets criminals roam
Federal ban halts Argus facial-recognition program
POTUS PULLS THE PLUG on the spy cams 🔌

social.text: freedom won today. mark this date.

choices.advisor: You protected privacy at the cost of a tool every police chief from Memphis to Baltimore wanted.

title: Race China to Mars?
briefing: China has announced a crewed Mars landing for 2033, and NASA says it can beat Beijing there — but only with a $390 billion moonshot and a timeline that shaves safety margins thin. A failure would mean dead astronauts on global television. Do you pour the treasury into planting the flag first, or fund a slower, safer program and risk finishing second in the history books?

choices.label: Go all-in to win
choices.result: You commit a generation's ambition to the red planet; the world watches, the deficit groans, and the countdown to 2033 begins.

news.headline: President orders crash program to beat China to Mars
Bold bet could plant our flag on another world
Billions for Mars while Flint still needs pipes
USA vs. CHINA — RACE TO THE RED PLANET 🚀🔴

social.text: an american flag on mars under your watch? that's immortality.
we're rushing humans 140 million miles to win a PR war. people will die.

choices.advisor: Beat Beijing and you're in the history books forever. Lose a crew and you're there for a different reason.

choices.label: Fund a slower, safer path
choices.result: You back a methodical program built to keep crews alive; the engineers in Houston exhale as Beijing grabs the headlines.

news.headline: President chooses caution over a reckless space sprint
Timid timeline cedes the high frontier to Beijing
NASA Mars program funded on measured, safety-first schedule
POTUS lets CHINA get to MARS first 😴

social.text: slow and alive beats fast and dead. good call.

choices.advisor: Nobody remembers who came in second — but everybody remembers a disaster you avoided.

title: Edit the Human Genome?
briefing: A Johns Hopkins team has perfected embryonic gene editing that could erase sickle cell, Huntington's, and Tay-Sachs before birth — and, with the same tool, let parents select for height, looks, or IQ. Georgetown ethicists warn you're one FDA approval away from a designer-baby market only the rich can afford. Do you greenlight the cures and the slippery slope, or restrict the breakthrough and leave preventable suffering on the table?

choices.label: Approve therapeutic editing
choices.result: Families with Huntington's bloodlines weep with relief, while critics insist the line between cure and enhancement won't hold.

news.headline: FDA approves embryo editing to wipe out inherited disease
Medical miracle: America leads the world in genetic cures
Sickle cell cures today, designer babies tomorrow?
SCIENTISTS are EDITING BABIES and POTUS said YES 🧬👶

social.text: my daughter won't inherit the huntington's that killed my mother. thank you.
the enhancement ban is a speed bump, not a wall. watch.

choices.advisor: You're curing the uncurable — and opening a door you may not be able to close.

choices.label: Impose a moratorium
choices.result: You freeze human editing pending a Geneva ethics framework; safety advocates approve while desperate families book flights to clinics in Singapore.

news.headline: President hits pause on rewriting human life
Moratorium sends researchers — and cures — to Shanghai
Federal moratorium halts human embryo editing
POTUS SLAMS THE BRAKES on franken-babies 🛑🧬

social.text: every month we wait, families suffer. this is cruel.

choices.advisor: You bought time to think — at the price of cures Hopkins could have started today.

title: The Mother of All Breaches
briefing: Hackers have drained the records of 240 million Americans — Social Security numbers, health files, biometrics — from Meridian Data Systems, a federal contractor in Reston, Virginia. The crew, calling itself BlackHarbor and traced to servers in St. Petersburg, demands $75 million or it dumps everything online. Do you pay to make it disappear and reward the criminals, or refuse, harden the system, and brace the public for the fallout?

choices.label: Refuse and go public
choices.result: You won't fund criminals; BlackHarbor dumps the files, panic spikes, but allies from London to Tokyo respect a government that doesn't pay.

news.headline: President refuses ransom after historic Meridian breach
No deals with hackers — a firm stand on principle
240 million Americans left exposed by contractor failures
YOUR ENTIRE LIFE just LEAKED online 😱💀

social.text: never pay. ever. this is the right move even if it hurts.
my whole family's data is on some server in st. petersburg. i'm furious.

choices.advisor: Pay and BlackHarbor comes back for more. Refuse and you eat the chaos now. There's no clean exit.

choices.label: Quietly pay the ransom
choices.result: The files vanish and the panic fades, but a Senate staffer leaks that you wired $75 million to the crew that attacked the nation.

news.headline: Washington quietly wired $75 million to hackers
President funds a cyber-cartel, invites more attacks
Meridian breach contained after reported ransom payment
POTUS PAID THE HACKERS in secret 🤫💰

social.text: you just put a bounty on every federal system. unbelievable.

choices.advisor: You stopped the bleeding and painted a target on every server we own.

title: Quantum Cracks the Codes
briefing: Physicists at Sandia National Laboratories have cracked RSA-2048 in eleven hours — the encryption guarding banks, state secrets, and most of the internet. Announce it and you spark a global scramble — and hand Beijing a roadmap; bury it and the quantum lab in Hefei may get there first while citizens stay defenseless. Do you go public to force an emergency upgrade, or weaponize the secret advantage in the shadows?

choices.label: Disclose and lead the upgrade
choices.result: You warn the world and rush everyone onto NIST's quantum-proof standards; markets wobble in the transition, but you own the high ground.

news.headline: President reveals quantum threat, leads global crypto overhaul
A rare act of transparency to protect the public
Why hand Beijing a roadmap to our breakthrough?
EVERY PASSWORD ON EARTH is now USELESS 🔓😱

social.text: disclosing instead of hoarding the weapon. that's leadership.

choices.advisor: You traded a secret superweapon for the world's trust and a head start on the fix.

choices.label: Keep it secret, exploit it
choices.detail: Read Moscow's and Beijing's traffic while they're blind.
choices.result: Your analysts feast on the cables of every adversary, but your own citizens stay exposed and one leak from catastrophe.

news.headline: America gains a decisive, silent edge over its rivals
Government sits on a flaw that endangers its own people
Sandia quantum capability reportedly kept classified
POTUS can read EVERYONE'S secrets now 👀🔑

social.text: hoarding a flaw that breaks our own banks too? insane gamble.

choices.advisor: An intelligence goldmine — sitting on a vulnerability that could detonate at home.

title: Fatal Crash, Robot Wheel
briefing: A Waypoint Autonomy robotaxi struck and killed Maria Delgado, a 34-year-old nurse, in Chandler, Arizona — and the footage is everywhere. Federal data shows autonomous fleets crash at a quarter of the human rate; the industry begs you to keep the rollout alive while the Delgado family demands every robot car off the road. Do you stand by the technology that saves more lives on net, or ground the fleet until public trust returns?

choices.label: Keep the fleets rolling
choices.detail: Tighten NHTSA rules, don't halt the rollout.
choices.result: You let the cars keep driving on the data; the safety stats hold, but the Delgado family becomes the face of a furious backlash.

news.headline: President backs the tech that saves thousands of lives
Robots stay on the road as Chandler buries a nurse
Autonomous fleets to continue under tighter NHTSA rules
KILLER ROBOT CARS still ALLOWED to drive?! 🚗💀

social.text: humans kill 40,000 a year on our roads. the data is on the president's side.
tell the data to maria's mother. shameful.

choices.advisor: The math says you're saving lives. The cameras say you're defending the machine that killed Maria Delgado.

choices.label: Ground the fleet
choices.result: You pull every autonomous vehicle off the road pending review; the Delgados thank you as the industry warns the pause itself will cost lives.

news.headline: President halts robot cars to protect human life
Knee-jerk ban stalls a life-saving revolution
Self-driving fleets grounded pending federal safety review
POTUS YANKS the killer robo-cars off the ROAD 🛑🚗

social.text: a president who listens. thank you for stopping them.

choices.advisor: You answered the grief — and the safety researchers say the pause will cost lives you'll never see.
